title: Hedge
author: Jane Delury
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "Hedge" book by Jane Delury?
Options:
A. Spreading butter on his bread, he kept his eyes on his plate instead of looking at her. He is also uncomfortable around me, Maud thought. Even more so, she said aloud. I'm indifferent. It was as if birthing Ella gave me a new heart, which expanded with Louise. But the heart that loved Peter dwindled to an empty space. I thought mine was empty too, Gabriel said. His eyes rose from the bread and locked onto hers. I ponder you frequently, he said. I ponder you frequently too, Maud said. The waiter delivered their main courses, diverting them from the flush warming her face.
B. As he buttered his bread, he didn't glance up at her. He feels exposed with me too, Maud deduced. More so than that, she uttered. I don't care. It's as if having Ella gave me a new heart, which grew larger with Louise. But the heart that cherished Peter shriveled to a void. I assumed mine was a void too, Gabriel said. His eyes lifted from the bread dish and met her gaze straight on. I think a lot about you, he said. I think a lot about you too, Maud said. The waiter set down their entrees, a welcome distraction from the heat burning her cheeks.
C. He spread butter on his bread, keeping his eyes downcast instead of looking at her. He is also insecure around me, Maud considered. Even more than that, she stated. I'm indifferent. It's like when I had Ella, I developed a new heart, which expanded with Louise. But the heart that loved Peter diminished to a hole. I thought mine was a hole too, Gabriel said. His eyes rose from the bread plate and stared directly into hers. I contemplate you frequently, he said. I contemplate you frequently too, Maud said. The waiter brought their main courses, a welcome diversion from the warmth flushing her face.
D. He was buttering his bread, not looking up at her. He feels vulnerable with me too, Maud thought. Worse than that, she said. I dont care. Its as if when I had Ella, I grew a new heart, which got bigger with Louise. But the heart that loved Peter shrank to a pit. I thought mine was a pit too, Gabriel said. His eyes lifted from the bread plate and met hers squarely. I think about you a lot, he said. I think about you too, Maud said. The waiter set down their entres, a welcome distraction from the heat consuming her face.
Answer:

His eyes rose from the bread and locked onto hers. I ponder you frequently, he said. I ponder you frequently too, Maud said.
